myPay.title: myPay — Payment Middleware
myPay.description: Enterprise middleware for Regione Lombardia's payment ecosystem. Acts as a bridge between the regional platform and pagoPA, the Italian public payment system, orchestrating 40+ SOAP operations.

pointers.0: Implemented dynamic routing middleware that dispatches 40+ SOAP operations between regional systems and the pagoPA platform.
pointers.1: Designed OAuth2 + JWT authentication flow with automatic credential rotation and token refresh.
pointers.2: Integrated circuit breaker pattern (Resilience4j) and retry mechanisms to ensure fault tolerance in payment processing.
pointers.3: Built a centralized HTTP service layer with connection pooling, timeout management, and request logging.
pointers.4: Sole developer responsible for architecture, implementation, testing, and deployment of the entire middleware.

petFarmacy.title: Pet Farmacy — SaaS Platform
petFarmacy.description: Multi-tenant veterinary pharmacy SaaS with subscription management, WhatsApp notifications, and atomic quota system. Built as a monorepo with Turborepo.

pointers.0: Developed a multi-tenant architecture with Prisma v7 and PostgreSQL, isolating data per pharmacy while sharing infrastructure.
pointers.1: Implemented Stripe subscription system with tiered plans, usage quotas, and automatic billing.
pointers.2: Built real-time WhatsApp notification system via Twilio for prescription alerts and refill reminders.
pointers.3: Full-stack Next.js 15 app with App Router, Server Components, shadcn/ui, and Tailwind CSS v4.
pointers.4: Mobile companion app built with React Native + Expo for on-the-go pharmacy management.

food4You.title: Food4You — Food Delivery
food4You.description: Real-time food delivery mobile application with live order tracking, built with React Native and Socket.io.

pointers.0: Built cross-platform mobile app (iOS/Android) with React Native 0.79 and Expo 53.
pointers.1: Implemented real-time order tracking with Socket.io WebSockets for live delivery updates.
pointers.2: Designed role-based system: customers place orders, restaurants manage menus, and delivery personnel track routes.
pointers.3: Integrated secure authentication with Expo SecureStore and JWT tokens.

beachGo.title: BeachGo — Beach Management
beachGo.description: Mobile application for managing beach umbrella and structure reservations, streamlining tourist beach operations.

pointers.0: Built with React Native + Expo for both iOS and Android platforms.
pointers.1: Designed interactive beach map with drag-and-drop umbrella reservation system.
pointers.2: Implemented seasonal booking system with recurring reservations and availability calendar.
pointers.3: Backend API with Node.js, Express, and PostgreSQL for reliable booking management.

control.title: Control — Business Ecosystem
control.description: Comprehensive business management ecosystem with 1,100+ commits built solo. Monolithic Java backend with Angular frontend.

pointers.0: Architected a monolithic Spring Boot backend with Hibernate ORM managing 737+ Java files.
pointers.1: Built an Angular 12 dashboard with Material UI 5 for managing multiple business dimensions.
pointers.2: Designed modular feature-based architecture allowing independent development of business modules.
pointers.3: Implemented comprehensive role-based access control (RBAC) with granular permissions.

europCar.title: EuropCar — Car Rental
europCar.description: Vehicle rental management system built with Java/Spring Boot, handling reservations, fleet tracking, and customer management.

pointers.0: Developed with Java + Spring Boot MVC, JSP templates, and jQuery for the frontend layer.
pointers.1: Managed Oracle 10g database with complex queries for fleet availability and reservation scheduling.
pointers.2: Deployed on Apache Tomcat with Maven-based build pipeline.
pointers.3: Implemented vehicle lifecycle tracking from acquisition to rental and maintenance.

debtManagement.title: Debt Management
debtManagement.description: This application arose from the recurring need to manage debts efficiently.

pointers.0: The application has the ability to grant permissions or not, depending on who created the debt.
pointers.1: It features an authentication system. Only users who create debts with others must be registered users of the application and be direct contacts through implemented friend requests.
pointers.2: The application has a statistics system where the user can see, both globally and for specific debts, what the balance of their debts is.
pointers.3: Each debt has note systems, payments already implemented, among other features.

sellManagement.title: Sell Management
sellManagement.description: This application arose from the need to manage a business focused on selling items and printing photos and documents on paper.

pointers.0: The application, among other features, includes a worker management system. These employees are registered in the application and maintain a record, not only of their personal data, but also of all their achievements throughout their time in the business.
pointers.1: It has a daily sales record that includes information about the day's workers, sales, card payments, salary payments, and much more.
pointers.2: It features a note system to keep track of customer orders, completed orders, and various other details.
pointers.3: It has an inventory control section where a strict monitoring is kept on the availability, both in the business and in storage.
pointers.4: It has route controls, where registered workers only have access to specific parts of the application.
pointers.5: It features a system that, upon uploading an Excel file provided by a desktop program used in the local setup, can process all that data in the backend, saving the formatted information in the database for display on the webpage.
pointers.6: Additional details make this webpage a quite comprehensive platform.

restaurant.title: Restaurant
restaurant.description: This application arose from the need to manage a restaurant. The application has different features such as inventory management, orders, and more.

pointers.0: The application has a login and registration system. Only users who are logged in to the application can access the general management functions.
pointers.1: It also has an editing system, order records, billing systems, and admin panel where the administrator can manage all the entries in the application, as well as general statistics.
pointers.2: The application has a statistics system where the user can see, both globally and for specific orders, what the balance of their orders is.
pointers.3: Each order has note systems, payments already implemented, among other features.
pointers.4: It has an inventory control section where a strict monitoring is kept on the availability, both in the business and in storage.
pointers.5: It has route controls, where registered users only have access to specific parts of the application.
pointers.6: It has an implementation of artificial intelligence for the creation of menus, orders, and other features.
